channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Utes drop fourth straight to #3 Iowa State, 82-59
item.description: AMES, Iowa (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes gave #3 Iowa State a game, but the Cyclones overpowered the Utes down the stretch for an 82-59 victory, handing the Utes their fourth straight defeat. Curtis Jones had 23 points off the bench to lead Iowa State to its 10th straight win.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Penn State, Notre Dame arrive at Orange Bowl with a bigger game in mind
item.description: In an era that is now long gone, a trip to the Orange Bowl meant a break from the rough weather up north and the capper to a successful season that might even end with a championship trophy in the case.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Giants agree to deal with Justin Verlander: reports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants are adding former MVP Justin Verlander to their pitching staff, multiple outlets reported Tuesday. The team reportedly agreed to a one-year deal with the pitcher. Verlander, 41, is one of the best pitchers of the 2000s and a future Hall of Famer.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Kessler celebrating girlfriend's Miss America title
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Walker Kessler has played basketball in front of thousands of fans for the Utah Jazz. But he couldn't have been more stressed Sunday night when his girlfriend, Miss Alabama Abbie Stockard, was a finalist for Miss America.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Raiders fire coach Antonio Pierce after he goes 4-13 in lone full season
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — Antonio Pierce stood behind a lectern and took questions during an end-of-season news conference, even though his future as the Las Vegas Raiders' coach remained very much in question.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dolphins’ Tyreek Hill ‘never’ requested a trade in meeting with team brass, GM says
item.description: Miami Dolphins general manager Chris Grier and head coach Mike McDaniel met with star receiver Tyreek Hill on Monday after he indicated following Sunday's loss that he is ready to move on.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California to consider law banning trans athletes from girls' sports after countless scandals cause outrage
item.description: California State Assemblymember Kate Sanchez will propose the Protect Girls’ Sports Act to the state legislature after a year plagued by scandals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giants legend Eli Manning backs team's decision to stick with Brian Daboll, Joe Schoen despite woeful season
item.description: Fans might not like it, but former New York Giants quarterback Eli Manning is backing the team's decision to stick with head coach Brian Daboll and general manager Joe Schoen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College Football Playoff picks: Breaking down the (NFL Draft) talent disparity in the semifinal matchups
item.description: The Buckeyes are the team to beat with two rounds remaining and should handle Texas easily. The Notre Dame-Penn State affair is almost too close to call.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: UConn guard Paige Bueckers has a left knee sprain, could return in a week
item.description: STORRS, Conn. (AP) — UConn guard Paige Bueckers has a left knee sprain, avoiding a serious injury. She will miss Wednesday's game against Xavier. Bueckers injured the knee in the third quarter of UConn's win over Villanova on Sunday. Coach Geno Auriemma said she may miss a few games.
item.dc:creator: JIM FULLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black coaches have shot at college football title a generation after that milestone was hit in hoops
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — In college basketball, the scene of an African American coach cutting down the nets on their way to a national title is not new, even if it is still a relative rarity.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Titans, with No. 1 draft pick, fire general manager Ran Carthon
item.description: The Tennessee Titans fired general manager Ran Carthon after the team was 9-25 over his two seasons. The Titans hold the No. 1 overall pick in the 2025 NFL Draft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers stars reflect on how season badly derailed
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (KRON) -- As the San Francisco 49ers packed up their lockers at Levi's Stadium on Monday, players told reporters about how they will move forward into next season. Tight end George Kittle summed up the season as "disappointing.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Utah State cracks the top-25 rankings
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a 14-1 start, the Utah State basketball team is back in the top-25. The Aggies check in at No. 25 in both the AP Top 25 and USA Today Coaches Poll. Utah State is 4-0 in Mountain West play, under first-year USU head coach Jerrod Calhoun.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Browns fire offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and O-line coach Andy Dickerson after 3-14 season
item.description: Hours after finishing 3-14 with a loss in the finale of a season that was expected to carry into the playoffs, Cleveland coach Kevin Stefanski fired offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and offensive line coach Andy Dickerson.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Runnin' Utes get run over by Texas Tech, 93-65
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It may be a long first season in the Big 12 Conference for the Utes basketball team. Darrion Williams scored 19 points with nine assists, Federiko Federiko added 14 points on 7-of-9 shooting — including five dunks — on Saturday, and Texas Tech beat Utah 93-65.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James breaks Michael Jordan's record for 30-point games with his 563rd
item.description: LeBron James broke Michael Jordan's NBA record for 30-point games during the Los Angeles Lakers' victory over Atlanta on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers sign star out of Korean baseball league
item.description: If you thought the Los Angeles Dodgers would slow down on player signings because they already assembled a world champion roster, then you were wrong. According to the team on Friday, the Dodgers have added one of this offseason's most sought-after prizes: Korean star second baseman Hyeseong Kim.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: St. Pete Pier hosts 'Raise the Flags Friday' ahead of Bucs' regular season finale
item.description: The Buccaneers are counting down to the finale of their regular season. Sunday's home game against the New Orleans Saints is a must-win for the team to secure a spot in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah HC storms back at Calgary to end five-game skid
item.description: CALGARY (ABC4 Sports) - Lawson Crouse scored twice, and the Utah Hockey Club rallied for a 5-3 win against the Calgary Flames at Scotiabank Saddledome on Thursday to end a five-game losing skid.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Derrick Rose to open pop-up flower shop in the Loop Thursday
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Former Chicago Bull and NBA MVP Derrick Rose is opening a pop-up flower shop for one day only in the Loop Thursday. According to a post on Rose's Instagram account, "Rose's Flower Shop" will be open from 12-4 p.m. at 350 North State Street.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl in New Orleans postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KTLA 5
